quiz.title: Unit 11 Quiz 8, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: BrГёnsted-Lowry Theory and Conjugate Pairs
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: BrГёnsted-Lowry Theory, Conjugate Acid-Base Pairs, Amphoteric Substances, Proton Transfer Reactions

questions.question: Which is a conjugate acidвЂ“base pair?
questions.explanation: HCl and ClвЃ» differ by one proton (HвЃє). When HCl donates a proton, it becomes ClвЃ», making them a conjugate acid-base pair.

questions.question: Which substance can act as both an acid and a base?
questions.explanation: Water (Hв‚‚O) is amphoteric вЂ” it can act as both an acid (donating a proton to become OHвЃ») and a base (accepting a proton to become Hв‚ѓOвЃє).

questions.question: A substance that can act as both an acid and a base is called:
questions.explanation: An amphoteric substance can act as both an acid and a base. Water is the most common example, but others include Al(OH)в‚ѓ and many metal hydroxides.

questions.question: Which of the following does not form an acidic salt?
questions.explanation: Hydrochloric acid (HCl) is a monoprotic strong acid. It does not form acidic salts because it has only one ionizable proton. Phosphoric acid (triprotic) forms salts like NaHв‚‚POв‚„ and Naв‚‚HPOв‚„, carbonic acid (diprotic) forms NaHCOв‚ѓ, and sulfuric acid (diprotic) forms NaHSOв‚„ вЂ” all are acidic salts.

questions.question: The chemical nature of calcium phosphate, which is the main component of tooth enamel, is:
questions.explanation: Calcium phosphate is amphoteric, meaning it can react with both acids and bases. This property is important for tooth enamel as it allows it to resist demineralization by acids and remineralization in the presence of saliva.

questions.question: Which of the following substances cannot act as both acid as well as a base?
questions.explanation: Protophilic substances are proton acceptors only, meaning they can only act as bases. In contrast, amphoteric, amphiprotic, and ampholyte substances are all capable of acting as both acids and bases under appropriate conditions.

questions.question: Which of the following is the strongest acid known?
questions.explanation: HClOв‚„ (perchloric acid) is one of the strongest simple acids, completely dissociating in water. HCN is a very weak acid, HCl is strong but weaker than HClOв‚„, and HNOв‚ѓ is strong but weaker than both HCl and HClOв‚„.

questions.question: The equivalence point in a titration is defined as:
options: When moles of acid equal moles of base
When the volume of titrant equals the volume of analyte
questions.explanation: The equivalence point in a titration is when the number of moles of titrant added is exactly equal to the number of moles of analyte present, according to the stoichiometry of the reaction. The endpoint is when the indicator changes color (A). pH = 7 only occurs at the equivalence point for strong acid-strong base titrations, not for all titrations (C). The volume of titrant equals volume of analyte (D) has no significance in titration.

questions.question: In the reaction NHв‚ѓ + Hв‚‚O в†’ NHв‚„вЃє + OHвЃ», what is the BrГёnstedвЂ“Lowry acid?
questions.explanation: Hв‚‚O donates a proton to NHв‚ѓ, forming OHвЃ» and NHв‚„вЃє. Since Hв‚‚O donates a proton, it acts as the BrГёnsted-Lowry acid.

questions.question: In the reaction NHв‚ѓ + Hв‚‚O в†’ NHв‚„вЃє + OHвЃ», what is the conjugate acid?
questions.explanation: NHв‚ѓ accepts a proton to become NHв‚„вЃє. Therefore, NHв‚„вЃє is the conjugate acid of the base NHв‚ѓ.

questions.question: Which species is the strongest BrГёnstedвЂ“Lowry acid?
questions.explanation: Hв‚ѓOвЃє (hydronium ion) is the strongest BrГёnsted-Lowry acid among these options. It readily donates protons to bases. NHв‚ѓ is actually a base, OHвЃ» is a strong base, and Hв‚‚O is neutral with weak acidic properties.

questions.question: Which theory explains acidвЂ“base reactions in non-aqueous solutions?
questions.explanation: BrГёnsted-Lowry theory defines acids as proton donors and bases as proton acceptors, which applies to all solvents and even gas-phase reactions, unlike Arrhenius theory which is limited to aqueous solutions.

questions.question: In the reaction Hв‚‚SOв‚„ + Hв‚‚O в†’ Hв‚ѓOвЃє + HSOв‚„вЃ», what is the conjugate base?
questions.explanation: Hв‚‚SOв‚„ donates a proton to become HSOв‚„вЃ». Therefore, HSOв‚„вЃ» is the conjugate base of the acid Hв‚‚SOв‚„.

questions.question: What happens to an acid after it donates a proton? It becomes a/its _____________
questions.explanation: When an acid donates a proton, it becomes its conjugate base. For example, HCl (acid) donates a proton to become ClвЃ» (conjugate base).

questions.question: Which pair represents a conjugate acidвЂ“base pair?
questions.explanation: NHв‚„вЃє and NHв‚ѓ differ by one proton. NHв‚„вЃє can donate a proton to become NHв‚ѓ, making them a conjugate acid-base pair.
